headline: **MLB Daily Recap: April 20, 2026**
article:
The Washington Nationals secured a 3-0 victory over the San Francisco Giants, with Andrew Alvarez pitching 4.1 innings of scoreless relief. Curtis Mead led the Giants' offense with a home run and 2 RBIs. The Pittsburgh Pirates took down the Tampa Bay Rays 6-3, with Mitch Keller pitching 7.0 innings and allowing 2 earned runs. Junior Caminero led the Pirates' offense with a home run and 1 RBI. The New York Yankees dominated the Kansas City Royals in a 7-0 shutout, with Ryan Weathers earning the win after 7.1 innings of scoreless relief. Trent Grisham led the Royals' offense with a 2-hit, 1-home run, 3-RBI performance.

Several players have been impacted by injuries and availability. Minnesota Twins right-handed pitcher Mick Abel has been placed on the 15-day injured list due to right elbow inflammation, effective retroactive to April 17, 2026. Houston Astros left fielder Joey Loperfido has been placed on the 10-day injured list retroactive to April 18, 2026, with a right quad strain. The Kansas City Royals have transferred right-handed pitcher James McArthur from the 15-day injured list to the 60-day injured list due to right elbow inflammation.

The Houston Astros made a move on April 19, 2026, acquiring shortstop Braden Shewmake in a trade. Shewmake joins the Astros after being dealt from the New York Yankees. The Los Angeles Dodgers traded right-handed pitcher Axel Perez to the Baltimore Orioles for right-handed pitcher Chayce McDermott. The New York Mets traded left-handed pitcher Richard Lovelady to the Washington Nationals for cash. The Arizona Diamondbacks traded right-handed pitcher Junior Fernández to the Milwaukee Brewers. The Philadelphia Phillies traded right-handed pitcher Griff McGarry to the Los Angeles Dodgers for future considerations and international bonus pool money.

top_news.title: **Giants Fall Short in Nation's Capital**
top_news.summary: The Washington Nationals secured a 3-0 victory over the San Francisco Giants, with Andrew Alvarez pitching 4.1 innings of scoreless relief. Curtis Mead led the Giants' offense with a home run and 2 RBIs.

top_news.title: **Pirates Take Down Rays 6-3**
top_news.summary: The Pittsburgh Pirates secured a 6-3 victory over the Tampa Bay Rays, with Mitch Keller pitching 7.0 innings and allowing 2 earned runs. Junior Caminero led the Pirates' offense with a home run and 1 RBI.

top_news.title: **Royals Fall to Yankees in Dominant 7-0 Shutout**
top_news.summary: The New York Yankees secured a convincing 7-0 victory over the Kansas City Royals, with Ryan Weathers earning the win after 7.1 innings of scoreless relief. Trent Grisham led the Royals' offense with a 2-hit, 1-home run, 3-RBI performance.

top_news.title: **Orioles Fall to Guardians in 8-4 Loss**
top_news.summary: The Baltimore Orioles suffered a tough defeat on the road, losing 8-4 to the Cleveland Guardians. José Ramírez led the Guardians' offense with two home runs and two RBIs.

top_news.title: **Twins RHP Mick Abel Lands on Injured List**
top_news.summary: Minnesota Twins right-handed pitcher Mick Abel has been placed on the 15-day injured list due to right elbow inflammation, effective retroactive to April 17, 2026.

top_news.title: **Astros Acquire Shewmake in Trade**
top_news.summary: The Houston Astros made a move on April 19, 2026, acquiring shortstop Braden Shewmake in a trade. Shewmake joins the Astros after being dealt from the New York Yankees.
